Dipodium squarrosum, also known as the Rough-leaved Dipodium, is a species of orchid that belongs to the genus Dipodium. This plant is reported to have various health benefits, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of Dipodium squarrosum include flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dipodium squarrosum include teas and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.

It is further classified as a dicotyledonous plant in the class Magnoliopsida. The genus Diporidium is also classified within the order Magnoliales. The classification of Diporidium squarrosum is an area of ongoing research and study.
